stemHtml: When flying in heavy precipitation, a pilot experiences communication issues. There is an increase in background noise and the pilot struggles to hear ATC. The pilot has previously flown this route multiple times and, when the weather has been clear, this has never happened. Other aircrafts seem to hear ATC fine. What is the most likely reason for the interference?

options.a: The aircraft has lost its earth point in its electrical system.
options.b: The batteries in your headset needs to be replaced.
options.c: The aircraft have lost multiple static wicks.
options.d: You are out of range from the ATC station.
